subject: english
learning_objectives: Identify the five grammatical functions of noun clauses

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: A noun clause is a subordinate clause that performs the same functions as a noun.

sections.heading: Functions of the Noun Clause
sections.content:
(i) As subject of a sentence:
  What he said is bitter.
  That he was insulted pained him a great deal.

(ii) As object of a verb:
  The cook gave us what we should eat.
  He told us that he would come.

(iii) As complement of the subject:
  Honesty is what we need.
  The important thing is that he has arrived.

(iv) As complement of a preposition:
  We call him what he likes.

(v) As object complement:
  The prize will go to whoever wins.
